Flowline Lead (Process Leader) - job post

Job details
Here’s how the job details align with your profile.

Job type


Full-time

 
Location


Derby
 
Full job description
Job Requirements

At Quest Global, it’s not just what we do but how and why we do it that makes us different. With over 25 years as an engineering services provider, we believe in the power of doing things differently to make the impossible possible. Our people are driven by the desire to make the world a better place—to make a positive difference that contributes to a brighter future. We bring together technologies and industries, alongside the contributions of diverse individuals who are empowered by an intentional workplace culture, to solve problems better and faster.

ROLE PURPOSE
To operate within the End-to-End solutions team. To own and manage an engineering process flowline set of deliverables (non-conformance, repair deviations, manual changes, changes in design, etc.) for Aero Engine Components to contractual Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). The role offers opportunities for developing skills, knowledge and experience in a blend of engineering and operations management in this exciting industry.

A Flow Line Lead (FLL) is required to work closely with Quest Global & Quest Defense engineers and the Customer and all elements of their Producibility business including technical points of contact(s). The FLL is expected to own, manage and drive the Flowline and supporting & steering internal and external stakeholders to deliver against agreed KPIs. This role also includes relationship building with specialist areas to understand bottlenecks, identify and deliver process improvements, drive Lean behaviors to enable on-time delivery and increased value.

PRINCIPAL RESPONSIBILITIES/ACCOUNTABILITIES

1. Vision, Focus and Direction

Own and drive the flowline to Lean principles and define the vision for improved customer value. Ensuring KPIs of asset Quality and Delivery are adhered to.
Management of ‘capacity bottleneck’ areas using pro-active planning and solutions to resolve actual and potential delivery concerns through short interval control.
Work with specialist functions and external stakeholders to develop SLA turn-around-times for engineering process artefact review and approval to deliver standard end to end process lead times.
Develop working relationships with specialist functions and external stakeholders to identify opportunities to embed a ‘pull system’ to improve output and customer value.
Manage team of coordinators ensuring day-to-day delivery and team performance.
Management of build critical priorities within the team without affecting flow - operate and demonstrate FIFO working arrangement.
Own Flowline visual management performance measured by agreed KPI and reviewed with the customer on a regular basis.
2. Coach and Problem Solver

Coach and develop Quest Global, Quest Defense, and customer teams on Lean principles and behaviors and support them on problem solving to support the delivery of the flowline.
3. Relationship building skills

Build relationships with specialist areas within and outside of the corresponding customer department to ensure the Flowline seamlessly delivers. Ensure flowline lessons learned and best practices are shared with the Principal Flowline Lead (Engineering Process Program Manager) and rolled out to other flowline departments.
4. Strive for Improvement

Identify improvement opportunities within the end-to-end process. Develop and own a visual management system, i.e. SQDCP (safety, quality, cost, delivery, people) board, which will drive continuous improvement.
5. Customer Focus and Value

Focus and value are to be measured through KPIs and reviewed on a regular basis. Deliver regular waste reduction initiatives such as over processing or repetition as well as cost reduction for each engineering process artefact through waste elimination.

We wholeheartedly believe in the diversity of thought that comes with fostering a culture rooted in respect, where everyone belongs, is valued, and feels inspired to share their ideas. We know embracing our unique differences makes us better, and that solving the worlds hardest engineering problems requires diverse ideas, perspectives, and backgrounds. We shine the brightest when we tap into the many dimensions that thrive across over 21,000 difference-makers in our workplace.


Work Experience

Required Skills & Experience:

1. Bachelors degree or equivalent.

2. Experience with the engineering process they will have ownership over (non-conformance, repair deviations, manual changes, changes in design, etc.)

3. Experience leading projects directly in the aero-engine industry (through direct OEM experience) or the engineering services industry


Desirable Skills & Experience:

1. An excellent technical understanding of gas turbine functionality and operability of major components.

2. Experience leading & managing engineering teams (people management)

3. Experience working with global engineering teams, understanding cultural differences and relationship building with colleagues in other countries.
shortCompanyReason: Engineering services provider with 25+ years’ experience and a focus on solving complex problems.
shortRoleReason: Lead an engineering flowline, manage KPIs, and drive Lean process improvement for aero engine components.
companySummary: Quest Global is an engineering services provider with over 25 years of experience, focused on solving complex engineering problems through diverse teams and an intentional workplace culture.
roleSummary: The Flowline Lead will own and manage engineering process flowline deliverables for aero engine components, coordinate stakeholders, manage a team of coordinators, and drive Lean, KPI-led improvements.
headlineAttraction: Own a critical aero-engine engineering flowline and drive Lean improvements with customer-facing impact.
rolePurpose: To manage an engineering process flowline for aero engine components and deliver contractual KPIs through Lean, coordinated, end-to-end process control.
advertSummary: Quest Global is hiring a Flowline Lead in Derby to manage an end-to-end engineering flowline for aero engine components. The role focuses on KPI delivery, Lean working, stakeholder coordination, team leadership, and continuous improvement.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this role because it sits right where I do my best work: taking a complex process, making the flow visible, and keeping delivery under control when priorities shift. I’ve led operations in live, regulated and manufacturing settings where performance depended on clear ownership, good reporting and steady follow-through.
whyThisRole: This role fits the kind of work I enjoy most: owning an end-to-end flow, managing performance against KPIs, and removing friction in the process. I’m used to working across stakeholders, keeping delivery moving, and turning operational data into practical action.
companyPridePoints: 25+ years as an engineering services provider
diverse individuals and intentional workplace culture
coreResponsibilities: Own and drive the flowline to Lean principles
Manage capacity bottlenecks and delivery risks
Develop SLA turnaround times with specialist functions and stakeholders
Build relationships with internal and external stakeholders
Use FIFO working arrangements for priorities
Own visual management performance and customer reviews
Coach teams on Lean principles and problem solving
Share lessons learned and best practices
Develop and own an SQDCP board
Drive continuous improvement and waste reduction
essentialRequirements: Experience with the engineering process owned by the role
Experience leading projects in the aero-engine industry or engineering services industry
preferredRequirements: Technical understanding of gas turbine functionality and major components
Experience leading and managing engineering teams
Experience working with global engineering teams
Understanding of cultural differences and relationship building across countries
impliedStrategicGoals: roll out best practices across flowlines

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operations leader who brings structure, pace and control to complex delivery flow.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m looking at this role because it sits right where I do my best work: taking a complex process, making the flow visible, and keeping delivery under control when priorities shift. I’ve led operations in live, regulated and manufacturing settings where performance depended on clear ownership, good reporting and steady follow-through.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Quest Global stands out to me because the brief is clear about how the business works: solving difficult engineering problems, bringing together different teams, and backing that with an intentional culture. I also value the emphasis on diverse perspectives and a workplace where people are expected to share ideas and improve how things are done.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This role fits the kind of work I enjoy most: owning an end-to-end flow, managing performance against KPIs, and removing friction in the process. I’m used to working across stakeholders, keeping delivery moving, and turning operational data into practical action.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: flowline ownership, bottleneck control and delivery discipline in an engineering environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve already worked in a specialist manufacturing setting where delivery discipline, capacity control and resilience mattered while the business was scaling.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest proof of relevant manufacturing/engineering operations experience.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I led day-to-day operations in specialist manufacturing while keeping delivery disciplined through growth.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Cross-site continuous improvement across European theme parks
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Lean improvement, stakeholder coordination and visual performance management
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve led improvement work across multiple sites using operational data and frontline insight, which is close to the kind of coordination and standard-setting this role needs.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting evidence for Lean thinking, cross-site working and practical improvement.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve used operational data and frontline insight to improve execution across multiple sites.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: team leadership, workload control and service performance under pressure
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve managed and supported a team through business-critical work in a controlled environment, which gives me a solid base for leading coordinators and keeping priorities moving.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting evidence for people leadership and structured workload management.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led a team through critical delivery while holding service standards steady.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My reading of this role is that it needs someone who can own a flowline properly, not just monitor it. That means keeping delivery visible, handling bottlenecks early, working well with specialist teams and customers, and making Lean improvements that actually stick.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve run day-to-day operations in a specialist manufacturing environment where I had to keep production, people and customer requirements aligned while the business scaled.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can take ownership of a live operational flow and keep it stable under pressure.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: In NHS primary care I built stronger operational grip around service performance and improved patient-facing outcomes significantly.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role is KPI-led, so I’d bring a habit of tracking performance and acting on what the numbers show.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Use Lean thinking to remove waste and improve flow
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led cross-site improvement activity using operational data and frontline insight to strengthen execution standards.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is directly relevant to improving flow, reducing friction and standardising better ways of working.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Lead and support coordinators or a small team
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve coordinated workload allocation and training across a 12-person function and supported delivery in a high-control environment.
experienceMappings.relevance: This gives me a practical base for managing day-to-day team performance and keeping priorities clear.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Build relationships with specialist functions and stakeholders
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked across multiple sites and teams, using data and frontline feedback to align people around practical improvements.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role depends on steady stakeholder coordination, not just internal control.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Improve customer-facing turnaround and service value
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I improved patient experience and online reputation in a live service setting by tightening follow-through and operational control.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I can help improve the customer experience where process quality is visible to the client.

focusAreasToBring.title: Flow control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d focus on making the flow visible early, so I can spot bottlenecks, manage priorities and keep work moving without losing control of quality or delivery.

focusAreasToBring.title: Lean habits that stick
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d keep improvement practical and measurable, using simple routines and clear ownership rather than treating Lean as a one-off exercise.

focusAreasToBring.title: Stakeholder rhythm
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d build a steady working rhythm with engineers, coordinators and customer contacts so issues are raised early and decisions are easier to make.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of operations control, improvement delivery and people leadership that fits the shape of this role well. My background includes specialist manufacturing, regulated services and high-volume operational environments, so I’m comfortable working where performance depends on structure, pace and good judgement.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first months getting close to the flowline, understanding where work slows down, and tightening the way performance is tracked. I’d then focus on practical improvements to turnaround, bottlenecks and team rhythm, while building strong working relationships with the people who influence the flow.
companyHighlights: Quest Global has over 25 years of experience as an engineering services provider.
The advert places real emphasis on diverse teams and an intentional workplace culture.
The role is tied to aero engine components and customer-facing KPI delivery.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people are expected to be direct, accountable and collaborative. The emphasis on respect, shared ideas and different perspectives feels like the kind of setting where I can contribute well.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I’d learn the process in detail, map the main handoffs, understand the KPI picture and build relationships with the key engineers, coordinators and customer contacts.
I’d focus on the main bottlenecks, clarify priorities, tighten visual management and make sure the team has a clear rhythm for tracking delivery and exceptions.
I’d start pushing practical Lean improvements, agree a few measurable changes with stakeholders, and make sure lessons learned are being shared in a way that supports wider rollout.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring steady control, practical improvement and a strong bias for making complex work flow better.
closingProofPoints: I improved Google reviews from 2.09 to 4.51 in NHS primary care.
I led operations in specialist manufacturing during a scale-up phase.
I’ve coordinated cross-site improvement work across five major European theme park sites.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct aero-engine or gas turbine experience because it is not shown in the CV or evidence bank.
The strongest transferable evidence comes from specialist manufacturing, regulated operations and cross-site improvement leadership.
I kept company highlights limited to facts clearly stated in the advert.

examples.title: “Cross-site continuous improvement across European theme parks”
“Re-designed triage workflow thinking for duty doctor model”
